quoted_tweet.text:
$SOFI 今回のSoFi決算は、
業績はかなり良いのに株価は期待されすぎていて下がった😒

一番大事なのはここ。

SoFiはもう赤字フィンテックではなく、かなり稼げる金融プラットフォームになりつつある。

売上は 11億ドル。
前年比で ＋43%。
これはかなり強い。

しかも利益も出てる。

純利益は1億6,670万ドル。
これで 10四半期連続黒字。

つまり、もう一時的に黒字になっただけではなく、継続して利益を出せる会社になってきてる。

特に強かったのは、

会員数：1,470万人（＋35%）
プロダクト数：2,220万件（＋39%）

ここ。

SoFiは銀行・ローン・投資・クレカ・学生ローン・住宅ローンなど、いろんな金融サービスを1人の顧客に使わせるモデル。

だから会員が増えるだけでなく、1人が複数サービスを使うほど利益が伸びやすい。

今回も融資実行額が 122億ドル で過去最高

個人ローン、学生ローン、住宅ローンすべて強い。

さらに重要なのが利益率。

調整後 EBITDAマージン31%

これは簡単に言うと、

売上が増えた分が、かなり効率よく利益に変わってる

ってこと。

つまり、SoFiはただ売上を伸ばしてるだけではなく、ちゃんと利益体質も強くなってる。

預金も402億ドルまで増えてる。

これはかなり大事。

預金が増えると、SoFiは安いコストで資金を集められる。
そのお金をローンに回せるので、銀行としての収益力が上がる。

だから今回の決算の本質は、

会員増加 → 預金増加 → ローン拡大 → 利益増加

この流れがかなり綺麗に回っていること。

一方で、なぜ株価が時間外で9%下がったのか？

理由は業績が悪いからではない。

期待値が高すぎたから。

市場は良い決算だけでは満足せず、もっと強烈な上方修正やサプライズを期待してた可能性が高い。

あと、一部セグメントの構成や今後の成長ペースに慎重な見方が出た。

だから、

会社の中身は強い。
でも短期の株価は期待先行の調整。

という感じ。

まとめると、

かなり良い決算。
SoFiの稼ぐ力は明確に上がってる。
会員・預金・ローン・利益が全部伸びてる。
ただし株価は期待が高すぎて短期的に売られた。

一言で言うなら、

「会社は強い。株価は一旦冷やされた」

kept.text:
🔥 $SOFI Flow Heatmap

$1.94M in $20 calls for January 2027. Right next to it in 2028, -$4.57M in puts at the exact same strike. Someone is bullish on $20 by next year and someone else is paying serious money to define the downside there a year later.

The $15 calls for 2027 are building quietly underneath while the $17 row stays consistently red across near-term expiries, protection layered just above where price is trading now.

Two massive bets, same strike, opposite conclusions.

 Which one ages like wine and which one like milk? 👇

profile_bio.description: I enjoy drinking good wine as much as I do trading stocks; they also happen to both cost me money.

quoted_tweet.text:
🐋 $SOFI Whale Watch

The $22 calls went vertical after April 28 and hit $400K — the only bull line with any real motion on this chart. $32 calls added $150K, $18 calls recovering to flat. That's it for the bull side.

Everything else went negative. 
$15.5 puts at −$500K, 
$19 puts at −$220K, 
$16 puts at −$280K, 
$25 puts at −$300K, 
$20 puts at −$280K

Making for a full put ladder from $15 to $25 building since April 28, all still moving. Price at $16, sitting inside the put cluster.

Fresh bear flow across six strikes vs. one recovering call line. The $22 calls are an island.

What just changed for $SOFI? 👇

kept.text:
$sofi

Why is Sofi's stock price lagging?

This is not investment advice. 

With book value roughly $7, a reasonable valuation for Sofi as a bank not considering growth...the "it's just a bank" valuation would be over $20. If you reasonably account for growth it should have a multiple of about 4-4.5 or $28 to $31.50.

So why is it at $16?

There's an uncertainty discount of war and inflation.  I'd calculate that at about $4-$6 because of the unknown direction of interest rates. However, Sofi has proven it can handle an increasing interest rates. So, let's set that at $4, because higher rates would still lessen projected growth vs lower rates. 

Then there's some discount related to how AI effects job availability. That should be a ways off and we've actually seen a jump in the hiring of software engineers over the last year,  and those are the people that should be most directly affected.  That being said, overall displacement of workers negatively effects the economy so I'll say that's a  discount of maybe $1-$2.

But the majority of the discount (about $10) needs to be explained. 

And it's not the failure of the tech platform or not having a tech multiple.

That's a backward looking argument. A tech multiple is simply a higher multiple based on growth, cost structure and margin.

And with Sofi's projected  3-year growth, shouldn't we be even higher than $28?

Here's why there's a discount.  The market believes there's execution risk. 

People are disconnecting the stock price and trading from underlying business investment and growth. 

The reason I warned  in January that first quarter eps might be lower is because of the scope and breadth of their investments in new businesses - payments,  crypto, stablecoins and big banking and also offering those products through Galileo and focussing on South America as I detailed in the Trojan Horse series. 

Then after earnings, I said there would be a brutal stock decline (even before the war which was obviously negative), because even though they are growing their mature lending and lpb businesses at a great clip, I felt that institutional and other investors want to wait and see how their big bets on these new businesses will perform.

The cut back in credit cards was a bit more strategic in my mind similar to the shift of their best loans to their balance sheet (again in my mind) necessary to have offsets for these investment costs over the next 12 to 36 months primarily to smooth earnings.  And credit card are riskier then personal loans. 

They probably won't say that but that's what they are clearly doing and that's actually what I would do.

The ebitda numbers they could hit for stablecoins and big business banking are actually staggering but they need to execute on a lot of businesses and it will take time. 

Noto is investing for 3 years from now, not for the stock price today. And the market doesn't understand that and can't measure it. 

I've been fairly critical on analysts and their valuations, but to be fair,  most analysts can't value future businesses because it's hard to determine a discount rate for speculative returns on business you're just starting,  but you do get a hit for the expenses for those businesses because it's current spend. 

Think of it this way.. say you found a chicken restaurant that was printing money and then said only 20% of our market likes chicken.... you were going to buy it but now they say there's so much potential profit in also opening a steak restaurant and a lobster restaurant and a bar this year.... and we're investing in these concepts. 

You might hesitate to buy thinking can they really do that? When will those businesses be profitable? What if there's a chicken shortage at the same time they're incurring expenses.

So they actually refocus on selling chicken pot pies instead of whole chickens. 

The market is skeptical of the new concepts, but it is also totally underestimating the benefit of growing members and revenue.

$SOFI in the $16’s is wild to me.

They are one of the most consistent companies in the fintech sector, highly profitable and becoming even more profitable as time goes on.

Anthony Noto at the helm is steering the ship in the right direction.

Strong buy here. https://t.co/208qjvfKZs

kept.text:
$SOFI is at the same price as 5 years ago

But it is a completely different business

5 years ago:
• Revenue: $565.5M
• EPS: -$4.30
• Members: 1.85M
• Total products: 2.52M

Now
• Revenue: $3.91B
• EPS: $0.39
• Members: 14.7M
• Total products: 22.2M

SoFi went from a focused lending app to a digital bank + financial ecosystem:
• lending
• deposits
• investing
• financial services
• B2B tech infrastructure
• Loan Platform Business
• stablecoin / business banking initiatives

Is this an opportunity?
Or did the investment thesis shift?

tweet.text:
$SOFI WHEEL STRATEGY SETUP LOOKS TOO GOOD TO IGNORE

$SOFI has pulled back into the low-$16 range, and this is exactly where the wheel strategy starts to get interesting. Current price is around $16.12.

The plan is simple:

Sell puts every week while trying to get assigned near the $15 range.

That means you’re collecting cash upfront while waiting for a better entry.

And if you do get assigned?

You own $SOFI at a lower effective cost basis, then start selling covered calls to collect even more premium while aiming to get called away for a profit.

Why I like the risk/reward here:

✅ Stock has already pulled back around 50%
✅ Weekly option premium is attractive
✅ $15 area gives a cleaner entry zone
✅ Insiders have been buying, including CEO Anthony 
✅Q1 results, with adjusted revenue up 41% YoY and members up 35% YoY.

This is why the wheel works so well on names you actually wouldn’t mind owning.

Collect cash to enter.
Collect cash while holding.
Collect cash to exit.

$SOFI at these levels is one I’m watching closely.
Not financial advice.
